creator: void@f-m.fm
text:
A speedtest from my location to Secaucus, NY on ipv6:

✓ Test Server: [Custom] [2604:a00:50:14::2]:8080
✓ Latency: 39.7657ms Jitter: 10.913363ms Min: 36.060171ms Max: 72.505346ms
✓ Download: 50.04Mbps (used: 59.65MB) (latency: 37ms jitter: 1ms min: 36ms max: 39ms)
✓ Upload: 13.07Mbps (used: 15.58MB) (latency: 40ms jitter: 10ms min: 36ms max: 72ms)

A workaround at this time for things like freebsd-update that don't have -4 or -6 switches
is to set ip6addrctl_policy="ipv4" and reboot. Otherwise freebsd-update won't work in a dual stack environment
